shortDescription: IRCC family sponsorship · Inland & outland · PR pathway for spouses

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current IRCC spousal sponsorship fees (principal + right of permanent residence fee) on canada.ca before quoting.

alert.title: Genuine relationship evidence
alert.body: Officers scrutinise relationship genuineness. Weak or inconsistent evidence is a leading refusal factor—build a chronological, document-backed story.

about.label: Description
about.value: Family class sponsorship allowing a Canadian citizen or permanent resident to sponsor a spouse, common-law, or conjugal partner for permanent residence. Outland applications processed from abroad; inland if partner is in Canada.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Legally married or qualifying common-law/conjugal relationship · Sponsor meets income/residency requirements · Genuine relationship · Medical and security clearances

about.label: Inland vs outland
about.value: Outland: partner outside Canada, can visit during processing. Inland: partner in Canada; may qualify for open work permit while awaiting decision.

about.label: After approval
about.value: Partner receives COPR and completes landing. Maintain relationship genuineness obligations; misrepresentation has serious consequences.

eligibility.criterion: Sponsor is Canadian citizen or PR aged 18+
eligibility.note: Verify sponsor status and residency obligations

eligibility.criterion: Legally valid marriage or qualifying relationship
eligibility.note: Marriage certificate; common-law needs 12+ months cohabitation proof

eligibility.criterion: Sponsor not in default of prior sponsorship undertakings

eligibility.note: Both sponsor and applicant if required

redFlagsBanner: If refused for relationship genuineness, do not reapply with the same weak evidence. Rebuild a chronological, verifiable relationship narrative first.

redFlags.title: Weak relationship evidence
redFlags.description: Few photos, no communication logs, short relationship timeline, or inconsistent stories.
redFlags.fix: Chronological timeline; chat/call logs; joint finances; visit stamps; affidavits from family

redFlags.title: Marriage of convenience suspicion
redFlags.description: Large age gap without explanation, met only days before marriage, or prior failed sponsorships.
redFlags.fix: Honest narrative; explain how relationship developed; strong third-party support

redFlags.title: Incomplete sponsor eligibility
redFlags.description: Sponsor on social assistance, prior undertaking default, or not meeting residency for PR sponsors.
redFlags.fix: Verify sponsor eligibility checklist on canada.ca before fee collection

redFlags.title: Wrong stream (inland vs outland)
redFlags.description: Applied inland when partner cannot remain legally in Canada, or outland without travel plan clarity.
redFlags.fix: Assess partner's current status; choose stream with legal counsel if complex

redFlags.title: Missing or inconsistent forms
redFlags.description: IMM forms incomplete, dates mismatch across documents, or unsigned declarations.
redFlags.fix: Cross-check all dates; dual review before upload

redFlags.title: Undisclosed prior relationships or refusals
redFlags.description: Hidden prior marriages, refusals, or misrepresentation history.
redFlags.fix: Full disclosure; address changed circumstances in cover letter

faqs.q: Can we guarantee PR approval for a genuine marriage?
faqs.a: No. Even genuine couples can face requests for additional evidence or refusal if documentation is weak. We improve file quality but never promise approval.

faqs.q: Inland or outland—which is faster?
faqs.a: Timelines vary and change. Outland may allow partner to visit during processing; inland may offer open work permit eligibility. Assess case-by-case.

faqs.q: How much relationship evidence is enough?
faqs.a: No fixed amount—officers want a credible chronological story: how you met, communication, visits, marriage, shared life plans. Quality over quantity.

faqs.q: Can a sponsored spouse work while waiting?
faqs.a: Inland applicants may apply for open work permit after AIP stage in many cases. Verify current IRCC rules at time of counseling.

faqs.q: What if marriage is recent?
faqs.a: Recent marriages need stronger evidence—communication history before marriage, visit records, photos, and explanation of relationship development.

faqs.q: Does sponsor need minimum income?
faqs.a: Spousal sponsorship generally has no LICO requirement for spouse/partner/child, but sponsor must not be on social assistance (except disability) and must meet undertaking obligations.

faqs.q: What happens after refusal?
faqs.a: Review refusal letter carefully. Options may include appeal (IAD) for in-Canada refusals in some cases, or reapplication with stronger evidence.

faqs.q: Are common-law partners eligible?
faqs.a: Yes, if they have cohabited continuously for at least 12 months. Evidence of shared residence is essential.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Family class sponsorship allowing a Canadian citizen or permanent resident to sponsor a spouse, common-law, or conjugal partner for permanent residence. Outland applications processed from abroad; inland if partner is in Canada.

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Legally married or qualifying common-law/conjugal relationship · Sponsor meets income/residency requirements · Genuine relationship · Medical and security clearances

faqs.q: What is Inland vs outland for this service?
faqs.a: Outland: partner outside Canada, can visit during processing. Inland: partner in Canada; may qualify for open work permit while awaiting decision.

faqs.q: Important note on Inland vs outland?
faqs.a: Outland: partner outside Canada, can visit during processing. Inland: partner in Canada; may qualify for open work permit while awaiting decision.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC)

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: Partner receives COPR and completes landing. Maintain relationship genuineness obligations; misrepresentation has serious consequences.

faqs.q: Is Sponsor is Canadian citizen or PR aged 18+ required?
faqs.a: Verify sponsor status and residency obligations

faqs.q: Is Legally valid marriage or qualifying relationship required?
faqs.a: Marriage certificate; common-law needs 12+ months cohabitation proof

faqs.q: Is Genuine relationship evidence required?
faqs.a: Photos, communication, financial mingling, visits

faqs.q: Is Sponsor not in default of prior sponsorship undertakings required?
faqs.a: Sponsor not in default of prior sponsorship undertakings is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Medical exam completed required?
faqs.a: Panel physician after instructions

faqs.q: Is Police certificates as required required?
faqs.a: Based on residence history

faqs.q: Is Biometrics completed required?
faqs.a: Both sponsor and applicant if required

faqs.q: What is the Processing time?
faqs.a: 12–18m — Varies by stream and office

faqs.q: What is the Government fee?
faqs.a: CAD $1,150+ — + biometrics, medical

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 91% — ~82% industry benchmark

faqs.q: What is the Required docs?
faqs.a: 18 — + relationship evidence

faqs.q: What is the Consultancy fee?
faqs.a: See fee tab — + govt & third-party

faqs.q: What if the client has Weak relationship evidence?
faqs.a: Chronological timeline; chat/call logs; joint finances; visit stamps; affidavits from family

faqs.q: Why is Weak relationship evidence a problem?
faqs.a: Few photos, no communication logs, short relationship timeline, or inconsistent stories.

faqs.q: What if the client has Marriage of convenience suspicion?
faqs.a: Honest narrative; explain how relationship developed; strong third-party support

faqs.q: Why is Marriage of convenience suspicion a problem?
faqs.a: Large age gap without explanation, met only days before marriage, or prior failed sponsorships.

compliance: Spousal sponsorship requires licensed RCIC or lawyer for paid representation in Canada—confirm credentials
Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee PR approval; marriage alone does not ensure success
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, medical, and biometrics
proTips: Build a relationship timeline document before collecting forms
Cross-check every date across marriage cert, photos, and travel history
For recent marriages, include pre-marriage communication evidence
Verify sponsor eligibility before taking full fees
Save relationship evidence binder separately for RFI responses
postApproval: Send COPR and landing instructions to client in writing
Remind client of PR card application after landing
Explain sponsor undertaking period (typically 3 years for spouse)
Register for post-landing settlement briefing if offered

timeline.title: Counseling, sponsor eligibility check, fee agreement

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Arranged marriages need extra care—build pre-marriage communication evidence and family affidavits without appearing fabricated.

staffNotes.author: Quality
staffNotes.text: Mandatory dual review for all spousal files—date mismatches are the top internal QA finding.

quiz.question: What is a leading refusal reason in spousal sponsorship?
options: Passport colour
Weak or inconsistent relationship evidence
Hotel bookings
Language test scores
quiz.explanation: Officers must be satisfied the relationship is genuine.

quiz.question: Common-law partners must generally show:
options: 1 month cohabitation
12+ months continuous cohabitation
No evidence needed
Only wedding photos
quiz.explanation: IRCC requires at least 12 months of continuous cohabitation for common-law.

quiz.question: Can counselors guarantee PR for genuine marriages?
options: Yes, always
No, never guarantee approval
Yes, if married 5+ years
Yes, if sponsor is citizen
quiz.explanation: Firm policy: never guarantee immigration outcomes.

quiz.question: Inland sponsorship may allow the partner to:
options: Skip medical exam
Apply for open work permit in many cases while awaiting decision
Work without any permit immediately
Leave Canada permanently without impact
quiz.explanation: Inland stream has specific work permit options—verify current rules.

quiz.question: Before submission, counselors must verify:
options: Sponsor eligibility and complete relationship timeline
Only marriage certificate
Client's school marks
Hotel reservations in Canada
quiz.explanation: Sponsor eligibility and relationship narrative are foundational.

dos: Build chronological relationship timeline with verifiable evidence
Verify sponsor eligibility before fee collection
Cross-check all dates across forms and documents
Disclose all prior relationships, refusals, and marriages
Prepare for RFI with evidence binder ready
donts: Do not guarantee PR approval for any relationship type
Do not submit fabricated photos or communication logs
Do not hide prior failed sponsorships or refusals
Do not choose inland/outland without assessing partner's legal status
Do not quote fees without checking current IRCC fee page
mistakes: Few relationship photos with no context or dates
Date mismatches between marriage cert and application forms
Missing police certificates for required countries
Sponsor on social assistance without eligibility check
Generic relationship letter not matching actual timeline

applicant.summary: Inland/outland spousal sponsorship leads to open work permit (SOWP) options while application is in processing, if eligible.
details: Principal applicant may receive bridging open work permit in eligible inland cases.
Conditions printed on permit must be followed.
restrictions: Work only after valid permit issued — not during unauthorized status

spouse.summary: Canadian citizen/permanent resident sponsor does not need work authorization in Canada. Foreign partner receives open work permit when SOWP approved.
details: Dependent children may have separate study/work rules — assess age and status.

fullCostBreakdown.title: Full cost breakdown — Canada – Spouse / Partner Sponsorship
fullCostBreakdown.disclaimer: Indicative costs for counselor discussions only. Government fees, tuition, and living amounts change — verify on official websites before quoting clients. Exchange rates affect INR equivalents.

totals.label: Indicative first-year budget (excl. tuition band)
totals.value: Counselor to calculate from sections above
totals.notes: Always cross-check LOA/CAS/Offer of Place and official fee pages before client commitment.
